chunks.text: A Modeling and Design Environment for Sensor Networks A Modeling and Design Environment for Sensor Networks Researchers: Yang Zhao, Xiaojun Liu, Elaine Cheong, Sanjeev Kohli Advisor: Edward A. Lee In this project, we are interested in developing a mo

this project, we are interested in developing a model and design environment for sensor networks. Sensor networks are characterized as distributed, dense, large scale, dynamic, resource limited, unpredictable, real-time, data-centric (as contrast to

edictable, real-time, data-centric (as contrast to address-centric in traditional networks), etc. The uniqueness introduces research challenges to many fields, such as networking, operating systems, database, signal processing, software design, etc

database, signal processing, software design, etc. On the other hand, programming, debugging and testing these systems could be very difficult, time consuming and expensive. A modeling, simulation and design environment for these systems could be

and design environment for these systems could be very helpful for people developing and testing new algorithms and applications in various areas. As an early stage, Sanjeev Kohli and Xiaojun Liu have developed a sensor domain based on distributed

e developed a sensor domain based on distributed event semantics in Ptolemy II and implemented different channel models (such as wireless channel and sound channel) to support different sensing and communicating mechanism. Yang Zhao and Xiaojun Liu

communicating mechanism. Yang Zhao and Xiaojun Liu have been working on using higher order modeling techniques to capture dynamic structures in a set of applications. As the next step, we would like to continue our work in several directions: * Le

to continue our work in several directions: * Learn modeling and design demands that people desire from different fields and improve the expressiveness of the sensor domain. * Look for a good programming model for channels to address issues like

ramming model for channels to address issues like noise, collision, states, etc. * Study challenges for distributing the computation to massive sensor nodes, including synchronous/asynchronous, redundancy, unreliable, etc. and look for solutions fr

ndancy, unreliable, etc. and look for solutions from a top down perspective. * Provide flexible co-simulation mechanism for sensor network models and their working environment models. Last updated 11/20/03
